content.full_text:
May 19, 1989


Steve Swendiman
County Supervisors Association
of California
1100 K Street, Suite 101
Sacramento, CA  95814

                                        Re:  Your Request for Informal Assistance
                                             Our File No. I-89-296

Dear Mr. Swendiman:

     Thank you for providing the CSAC articles of incorporation.
I have reviewed them and it appears that CSAC may qualify as a
"bona fide educational, academic or charitable organization" for
purposes of Government Code Section 82030(b)(2).  In that case,
reimbursement for travel expenses and per diem paid by CSAC to
public officials would be exempt from the disclosure requirements
of the Act, if the officials had provided equal or greater
consideration for the payments.  Enclosed is a copy of a recent
advice letter concerning a similar question (Sonoda Advice Letter,
No. A-89-179).

     After you review the enclosed letter, I would be happy to
meet and discuss this matter further.  I will be on vacation until
June 12, but I will be happy to schedule a meeting when I return.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        [illegible]

                                        Kathryn E. Donovan
                                        General Counsel

KED:plh

Enclosure




428 J Street, Suite 800 ● P.O. Box 807 ● Sacramento CA 95804-0807 ● (916) 322-5660

ARTICLES OF INCORPORATION
OF
COUNTY SUPERVISORS ASSOCIATION OF CALIFORNIA

We, the undersigned, hereby form a nonprofit corporation
under the provisions of Title XII, Part IV, Division First of the
Civil Code of the State of California, and execute these articles
of incorporation which state:

1. The name of this corporation is
   COUNTY SUPERVISORS ASSOCIATION OF CALIFORNIA.

2. This is a nonprofit corporation which does not contemplate pecuniary gain or profit to the members thereof. This
corporation shall not have any capital stock. It is organized and
shall be operated exclusively for educational and public service
purposes.

3. The purposes for which it is formed are:

   (a) To foster periodical conventions or meetings
of the members of the various boards of county supervisors located
within the state, for the discussion and solution of problems
common to the counties thereof, and for the purpose of promoting
efficiency in county government, and for the reduction of the cost
of county government.

   (b) To foster and disseminate knowledge relating to
county government and the problems thereof by appropriate legal
means and to arouse greater interest and more active civic consciousness among the people as to the importance and significance thereof.

   (c) To assist in the promotion of legislation beneficial to county government and to oppose the passage of legislation
detrimental to the best interests of the counties, and to this end
among other things the Association may participate in the preparation and sponsorship of such legislation, may attend meetings and

ENDORSED
FILED
[...Office of the Secretary of State
[...] January 1945

[...IEGISLATURE] - [...] of State
[...]
County

conferencea relating thereto, and may through its duly authorised
representatives attend the sessions of the state legislature or the
United States Congress, or any committee thereof.

(d) To secure harmony of action in the various
counties of the state in matters which affect the rights and liabil-
ities of said counties, and to render technical, informational and
other services to the various counties of the state and to the state
for the improvement of county government and for the general welfare
of the people of the state.

(e) To maintain headquarters at Sacramento and to
employ a sufficient technical staff to assist the counties and to
carry out the purposes of the Association.

(f) To purchase, lease, or otherwise acquire, mort-
gage and otherwise encumber, and dispose of any and all kinds of
real and personal property necessary for carrying out the purposes
of the Association.

(g) To possess and exercise any and all such addi-
tional powers as are reasonably implied from the purposes hereinabove
enumerated and to do and perform everything necessary, suitable or
proper for, or incident and appropriate to, the accomplishment of
the said purposes, and generally to advance the educational and pub-
lic service work undertaken by the Association in any manner and by
any means within the limitations imposed by law upon nonprofit cor-
porations of a similar character.

4. The principal office for the transaction of the
business of this corporation is located in the County of Sacramento,
State of California.

5. This corporation shall have twenty-two (22) directors,
but the number of directors may be changed from time to time by
amendment of these articles of incorporation or by a duly adopted
amendment to the constitution and by-laws. The names and addresses
of the persons who are to act in the capacity of directors until
the selection of their successors are as follows:

NAME                                    ADDRESS

Harry Bertell, President,        Court House,       Oakland,     California

Wm. A. Smith, Vice President, 501 Hall of Records, Los Angeles, California

Roscoe L. Patterson,                              Visalia,        "

W. J. Buchanan,                  549 E. 18th St.,   Pittsburg,      "

W. R. Channon,                                    Live Oak,       "

W. Z. Davidson,                                   Fort Jones,     "

Jas. R. Garlick,                 Court House,      Sacramento,     "

Peter Gerks,                                      Bieber,         "

B. K. Graham,                    P.O. Box 964,      Brawley,        "

Ed. O. Kaehl,                                     Cloverdale,     "

Ernest H. Hodge,                                  Tuolumne,       "

Karl S. Hurlbut,                                  Independence,   "

George A. Janssen,               5004 Foothill Blvd., Oakland,      "

Roger W. Jessup,                 501 Hall of Records, Los Angeles,   "

Robert W. Lofever,                               Moorpark,       "

Edward T. Manouso,               703 Market St. 25,  San Francisco,  "

Ralph E. Kinahan,                Partrick Road,     Napa,           "

A. F. Noon,                      The Fort,         Taft,           "

Frank J. Rowe,                   250 Maiden Lane,   Grass Valley,   "

C. E. Steinegul,                                  Escalon,        "

R. E. Treutner,                  R.P.D. 2, Box 363, Hanford,        "

R. H. Yates,                     Route 1,          Chowchilla,     "

6. The authorised number and qualifications of the
members, the officers and board of directors and the different
classes of membership shall be as set forth in the constitution and
by-laws of this corporation. Neither the members nor the officers
or directors of this corporation shall be personally liable for the
debts, liabilities or obligations of the corporation.

7. The term of existence of this corporation shall be
perpetual, unless hereafter otherwise provided by law, or unless
the corporation is dissolved in the manner provided by law.

8. The title to and membership of all the corporate
property and of all money or property given or distributed to it
shall be vested in the corporation and shall be managed by the Board
of Directors for the purposes thereof.

9. The name of the existing unincorporated association,
which is being incorporated by these Articles of Incorporation is
the same as the name of this incorporation, to wit, COUNTY
SUPERVISORS ASSOCIATION OF CALIFORNIA.

IN WITNESS WHEREOF, the undersigned incorporators have
executed and entered into these articles of Incorporation, at
Sacramento, California, for the purpose of incorporating the unin-
corporated association known as the County Supervisors Association
of California.

Dated this 24 day of February , 1945.

Harry Bartell, President.

Frederic L. Alexander,
Executive Secretary.

STATE OF CALIFORNIA, }
County of Sacramento, } ss.

On this 22 day of February , 1945,

before me, Emma Dosbler , a Notary Public in
and for the said County and State, residing therein, duly commissioned and sworn, personally appeared HARRY BARTELL and
FREDERIC L. ALEXANDER, who, being sworn, each for himself, deposes
and says: that the said HARRY Bartell is the President of COUNTY
SUPERVISORS ASSOCIATION OF CALIFORNIA and that the said Frederic L.
Alexander is the Executive Secretary of COUNTY SUPERVISORS ASSOCIATION
OF CALIFORNIA. That COUNTY SUPERVISORS ASSOCIATION OF CALIFORNIA
is an unincorporated association and that said association has duly
authorized its incorporation. That Harry Bartell and Frederic L.
Alexander have executed these articles of incorporation in their
official capacity and by authority of such association.

Harry Bartell

Frederic L. Alexander.

Subscribed and sworn to before me this 22
day of February , 1945.

STATE OF CALIFORNIA, }
County of Sacramento } ss.

On this 24th day of February in the year one thousand nine hundred and forty-five
before me Emma Dosbler -- a Notary Public in and for the County of Sacramento,

personally appeared Harry Bartell and Frederic L.
Alexander - - - - - - - - - - - - - -

known to me to be the person S whose name S are subscribed to the
within instrument and they duly acknowledged to me that S by
executed the same.

IN WITNESS WHEREOF, I have hereunto set my hand and affixed
Official Seal the day and year in this certificate first above written

Emma Dosbler
Notary Public in and for the County of Sacramento, State of California

My Commission Expires Jan 8, 1949

STEVE SWENDIMAN
Deputy Director, Operations

[CSAC LOGO]

County Supervisors Association of California
916/441/4011
ATSS 473-3727
Home (916) 446-8051

1100 K Street, #101
Sacramento, CA 95814

✓ non-profit entity

officers are elected city offs,
travel on CSAC business
such as for annual mess
mtg, rep. cowen is bd as
member of CSAC, testify
before congress, etc.

Asked for adv in writing in 1984
check for letter to Dave break
Shasta County DA
